title: yosys-abc failed return code 127; libreadline6 vs libreadline7
body:
I [commented in #1028](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/issues/1028#issuecomment-627745567) on what I thought was a similar issue, but apparently it is unrelated, so I'm opening this new issue as [suggested](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/issues/1028#issuecomment-627792593).

## Steps to reproduce the issue

I'm installing `yoysys` on a fresh WSL Ubuntu 18.04.4 LTS system per the [instructions](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys#setup).

## Expected behavior

`yoysys` to run without error.

## Actual behavior

As noted in https://github.com/icebreaker-fpga/icebreaker-examples/issues/10#issuecomment-627049625 I am seeing this error using the most recent version of `yosys` 0.9+2406 (git sha1 27b7ffc7, gcc 7.5.0-3ubuntu1~18.04 -fPIC -Os):

```
yosys -ql 7seg_count.log  -p 'synth_ice40 -top top -json 7seg_count.json' 7seg_count.v
ERROR: ABC: execution of command "/usr/local/bin/yosys-abc -s -f /tmp/yosys-abc-zImuXR/abc.script 2>&1" failed: return code 127.
```

TL;DR: `yosys-abc` shows `libreadline6` is missing, but `libreadline7` is installed. How to:

- install `libreadline6`
or
- change dependency to `libreadline7`

`sudo apt-get install libreadline-dev` installs `libreadline7` but the most recent code in the master branch appears to be expecting `libreadline6`. _Older_ versions of `yosys` are expecting `libreadline7` and seem to work fine.

@daveshah1 [suggested](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/issues/1028#issuecomment-627792593): `ldd /usr/local/bin/yosys-abc` and indeed that's pretty enlightening, indicating a missing `libreadline.so.6`:

```
  $ ldd /usr/local/bin/yosys-abc
        linux-vdso.so.1 (0x00007fffddd41000)
        libreadline.so.6 => not found
        libpthread.so.0 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libpthread.so.0 (0x00007fb37e3e0000)
        libm.so.6 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libm.so.6 (0x00007fb37e040000)
        libdl.so.2 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libdl.so.2 (0x00007fb37de30000)
        librt.so.1 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/librt.so.1 (0x00007fb37dc20000)
        libstdc++.so.6 => /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libstdc++.so.6 (0x00007fb37d890000)
        libgcc_s.so.1 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libgcc_s.so.1 (0x00007fb37d660000)
        libc.so.6 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6 (0x00007fb37d260000)
        /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2 (0x00007fb37e800000)

$ yosys --version
Yosys 0.9+2406 (git sha1 27b7ffc7, gcc 7.5.0-3ubuntu1~18.04 -fPIC -Os)

$ apt list --installed | grep libread

WARNING: apt does not have a stable CLI interface. Use with caution in scripts.

libreadline-dev/bionic,now 7.0-3 amd64 [installed]
libreadline7/bionic,now 7.0-3 amd64 [installed,automatic]

```

In contrast, I have an older `yoysys` working well on another WSL system: 93ef516d compiled in April, using `libreadline.so.7` (note the above newer NON-working system complains about missing `libreadline.so.6`). Here are the details if the older, working system:

```
$ yosys --version
Yosys 0.9+2406 (git sha1 93ef516d, clang 6.0.0-1ubuntu2 -fPIC -Os)

$ which yosys
/usr/local/bin/yosys

$ ls /usr/local/bin/yosys -al
-rwxr-xr-x 1 root root 18385608 Apr 12 11:44 /usr/local/bin/yosys

$ ldd /usr/local/bin/yosys-abc
        linux-vdso.so.1 (0x00007fffd922d000)
        libreadline.so.7 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libreadline.so.7 (0x00007f0491d20000)
        libpthread.so.0 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libpthread.so.0 (0x00007f0491b00000)
        libm.so.6 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libm.so.6 (0x00007f0491760000)
        libdl.so.2 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libdl.so.2 (0x00007f0491550000)
        librt.so.1 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/librt.so.1 (0x00007f0491340000)
        libstdc++.so.6 => /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libstdc++.so.6 (0x00007f0490fb0000)
        libgcc_s.so.1 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libgcc_s.so.1 (0x00007f0490d80000)
        libc.so.6 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6 (0x00007f0490980000)
        libtinfo.so.5 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libtinfo.so.5 (0x00007f0490750000)
        /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2 (0x00007f0492000000)

$ apt list --installed | grep libread

WARNING: apt does not have a stable CLI interface. Use with caution in scripts.

libreadline-dev/bionic,now 7.0-3 amd64 [installed]
libreadline5/bionic,now 5.2+dfsg-3build1 amd64 [installed]
libreadline7/bionic,now 7.0-3 amd64 [installed]
```

So the key seems to be the difference between non-working machine:
```
libreadline-dev/bionic,now 7.0-3 amd64 [installed]
libreadline7/bionic,now 7.0-3 amd64 [installed,automatic]
```
and the working machine:
```
libreadline-dev/bionic,now 7.0-3 amd64 [installed]
libreadline5/bionic,now 5.2+dfsg-3build1 amd64 [installed]
libreadline7/bionic,now 7.0-3 amd64 [installed]
```
How did I get `libreadline5/bionic,now 5.2+dfsg-3build1 amd64 [installed]`? 

As mentioned in https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/issues/643 I tried:

```
sudo apt-get purge libreadline-dev --assume-yes
sudo apt-get install libreadline6-dev --assume-yes
```
but no luck. I then end up with only:
```
apt list --installed | grep libread

WARNING: apt does not have a stable CLI interface. Use with caution in scripts.

libreadline7/bionic,now 7.0-3 amd64 [installed,automatic]
```

Yes, it seems odd that  `sudo apt-get install libreadline6-dev` installs `libreadline7`, but here it is:

```
0 $ sudo apt-get purge libreadline-dev --assume-yes
Reading package lists... Done
Building dependency tree
Reading state information... Done
Package 'libreadline-dev' is not installed, so not removed
The following package was automatically installed and is no longer required:
  libobjc-5-dev
Use 'sudo apt autoremove' to remove it.
0 upgraded, 0 newly installed, 0 to remove and 0 not upgraded.


0 $ sudo apt-get install libreadline6-dev --assume-yes
Reading package lists... Done
Building dependency tree
Reading state information... Done
Note, selecting 'libreadline-dev' instead of 'libreadline6-dev'
The following package was automatically installed and is no longer required:
  libobjc-5-dev
Use 'sudo apt autoremove' to remove it.
Suggested packages:
  readline-doc
The following NEW packages will be installed:
  libreadline-dev
0 upgraded, 1 newly installed, 0 to remove and 0 not upgraded.
Need to get 0 B/133 kB of archives.
After this operation, 728 kB of additional disk space will be used.
Selecting previously unselected package libreadline-dev:amd64.
(Reading database ... 101287 files and directories currently installed.)
Preparing to unpack .../libreadline-dev_7.0-3_amd64.deb ...
Unpacking libreadline-dev:amd64 (7.0-3) ...
Setting up libreadline-dev:amd64 (7.0-3) ...
Processing triggers for install-info (6.5.0.dfsg.1-2) ...
```
and `yosys` still gives the same error.

If I copy the `/usr/local/bin/yosys*` and `/usr/local/share/yosys/` files from the working, April build machine to the non-working, current build machine... `yosys` works great. So the problem seems to be something changed in `yosys` since April.

The key to my broken `yosys` seems to be `libreadline.so.6` - but how to install this explicit version? Why doesn't the newer `libreadline7` meet the requirement? The [setup](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys#setup) indicates only `sudo apt-get install libreadline-dev`. But that, too installs `libreadline7`. 

I might have tried ` sudo apt-get purge libreadline7` but that was a bit frightening:

```
0 upgraded, 0 newly installed, 193 to remove and 0 not upgraded.
After this operation, 321 MB disk space will be freed.
``` 

Any help or suggestions will be appreciated. Thanks.
